canonical_action: isolate each shard's sync work in its own spawned subprocess, so a smaller, per-shard memory footprint can be reclaimed by the OS when that subprocess exits or recycles
closure_reason: spawning and supervising a subprocess per shard forces the daemon itself to become a process launcher/supervisor, adding IPC and lifecycle-management complexity disproportionate to the memory saved
measured_outcome: a prototype cut peak per-shard resident memory but added enough process-management overhead and operational complexity that it was rejected; revisit only if a lighter in-process route (pooling/releasing buffers) turns out insufficient
